root_text.sn13.10:1.2: “Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, himavā pabbatarājā parikkhayaṁ pariyādānaṁ gaccheyya, ṭhapetvā satta sāsapamattiyo pāsāṇasakkharā.
root_text.sn13.10:1.4: katamaṁ nu kho bahutaraṁ, yaṁ vā himavato pabbatarājassa parikkhīṇaṁ pariyādiṇṇaṁ yā vā satta sāsapamattiyo pāsāṇasakkharā avasiṭṭhā”ti?
root_text.sn13.10:2.1: “Etadeva, bhante, bahutaraṁ himavato pabbatarājassa yadidaṁ parikkhīṇaṁ pariyādiṇṇaṁ;
root_text.sn13.10:2.3: Neva satimaṁ kalaṁ upenti na sahassimaṁ kalaṁ upenti na satasahassimaṁ kalaṁ upenti himavato pabbatarājassa parikkhīṇaṁ pariyādiṇṇaṁ upanidhāya satta sāsapamattiyo pāsāṇasakkharā avasiṭṭhā”ti.
root_text.sn13.10:3.1: “Evameva kho, bhikkhave, ariyasāvakassa diṭṭhisampannassa puggalassa abhisametāvino etadeva bahutaraṁ dukkhaṁ yadidaṁ parikkhīṇaṁ pariyādiṇṇaṁ;
root_text.sn13.10:3.3: Neva satimaṁ kalaṁ upeti na sahassimaṁ kalaṁ upeti na satasahassimaṁ kalaṁ upeti purimaṁ dukkhakkhandhaṁ parikkhīṇaṁ pariyādiṇṇaṁ upanidhāya yadidaṁ sattakkhattuṁparamatā.
root_text.sn13.10:3.4: Evaṁ mahatthiyo kho, bhikkhave, dhammābhisamayo, evaṁ mahatthiyo dhammacakkhupaṭilābho”ti.

translation_text.sn13.10:1.2: “Mendicants, suppose the Himalayas, the king of mountains, was worn away and eroded except for seven pebbles the size of mustard seeds.
translation_text.sn13.10:1.4: Which is more: the portion of the Himalayas, the king of mountains, that has been worn away and eroded, or the seven pebbles the size of mustard seeds that are left?”
translation_text.sn13.10:2.1: “Sir, the portion of the Himalayas, the king of mountains, that has been worn away and eroded is certainly more.
translation_text.sn13.10:2.2: The seven pebbles the size of mustard seeds are tiny.
translation_text.sn13.10:2.3: Compared to the Himalayas, it’s not nearly a hundredth, a thousandth, or a hundred thousandth part.”
translation_text.sn13.10:3.1: “In the same way, for a noble disciple accomplished in view, an individual with comprehension, the suffering that’s over and done with is more,
translation_text.sn13.10:3.3: Compared to the mass of suffering in the past that’s over and done with, it’s not nearly a hundredth, a thousandth, or a hundred thousandth part, since there are at most seven more lives.
translation_text.sn13.10:3.4: That’s how very beneficial it is to comprehend the teaching and gain the vision of the teaching.”
